data.title: Wall Street falls as investors assess in-line PCE report; Marvell also weighs
data.description:
The stock market opened in the red as the July PCE report came in-line, while Marvell's results weighed on Nasdaq. 

S&P 500 (SP500) -0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.1%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.65%.

July core PCE (excludes food and energy): +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% and +0.3% prior, and +2.9% Y/Y vs. +2.9% consensus and +2.8% prior.

PCE price index: +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.3%  prior, and +2.6% Y/Y vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.6% prior.

The U.S. international goods trade deficit expanded more than expected.

Wholesale inventories rose 0.2% M/M in July, as expected, and retail inventories rose 0.2%.

Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares plunged 17% in early trade after its recent quarterly results and guidance were nothing short of a disappointment to Wall Street, as investors expressed concern over the company's future in the artificial intelligence spending boom.

Wall Street’s major averages finished higher on Thursday, as investors weighed a wave of economic data and Nvidia’s (NVDA) latest earnings, all as the S&P 500 surpassed 6,500 and marked yet another record close.

"Markets put in another decent performance yesterday, with the S&P 500 at a new record as positive data reassured investors about the near-term outlook. But even as equities hit new highs, investors remain heavily focused on the Federal Reserve’s independence, and today there’s going to be an emergency hearing about Lisa Cook’s position on the Board of Governors that’s due to start at 10am EST," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen. 

In more rate cut news, Fed Governor Christopher Waller said Thursday he favors beginning U.S. interest rate cuts next month and anticipates additional cuts over the next three to six months, aiming to “ease monetary policy and move it to a more neutral stance.”

On the economic side, the August Chicago PMI will be released during market open. The Chicago PMI is seen ebbing to 45.2 in the month versus 47.1 in July.

data.title: TXNM Energy shareholders approve Blackstone acquisition
data.description:
TXNM Energy (NYSE:TXNM) said shareholders voted overwhelmingly to approve Blackstone Infrastructure's $11.5B acquisition of the company at a special shareholders meeting held Thursday.

Under the terms of the proposed agreement, TXNM Energy (NYSE:TXNM) shareholders will receive $61.25 in cash for each share of TXNM Energy common stock held at closing.

The deal still requires regulatory approvals from the New Mexico Public Regulation Commission, Public Utility Commission of Texas, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, U.S. Department of Justice, Nuclear Regulatory Commission and Federal Communications Commission.

Subject to the approvals, TXNM Energy (TXNM) continues to anticipate that the deal closing will occur in H2 2026.

data.title: Abbott gets EU CE Mark for expanded use of Navitor TAVI system
data.description:
• Abbott (NYSE:ABT) announced that it has obtained the CE Mark in Europe for a broader use of its Navitor transcatheter aortic valve implantation system.
  • This new approval allows the treatment of patients suffering from symptomatic, severe aortic stenosis who are considered to be at low or intermediate risk for open-heart surgery.
  • Previously, in 2021, Abbott received the CE Mark for Navitor to treat individuals with symptomatic, severe aortic stenosis who faced high or extreme surgical risks.
  • The decision for this expanded indication was based on positive safety and effectiveness results from the VANTAGE study.

data.title: DeepSeek said to use some Huawei chips to train small AI models: report
data.description:
• Chinese technology firm DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) is using some of Huawei's processors to train smaller artificial intelligence models, as it looks to reduce its reliance on Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), The Information reported. 
  • DeepSeek, which stunned the world in January, had tested AI accelerators from Huawei, Baidu (BIDU) and Cambricon to train its models, the news outlet added, citing sources familiar with the matter. It eventually chose Huawei and is working with the Chinese telecom giant to use its Ascend processors for training and refining smaller versions of its R2 model, which has not yet been released.
  • DeepSeek is still using Nvidia's processors for its more powerful R2 models, the news outlet added. The R2 model has not yet been publicly released and has been reportedly delayed amid a setback with some of Huawei's chips.
  • DeepSeek, Huawei and Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Cambricon — which is seen as a Chinese alternative to Nvidia — reported a 4,000% surge in revenue and swung to profit in the first half of the year earlier this week.

data.title: Post Holdings announces sale of Pasta business; share repurchase authorization of $500M
data.description:
• Post Holdings (NYSE:POST) said on Friday that it has entered into a definitive agreement to sell the pasta business of 8th Avenue Food & Provisions to Richardson Holdings Limited. 
  • In the transaction, Richardson will pay $375M in cash and assume approximately $80M in leaseback financial liabilities. 
  • Post acquired 8th Avenue on July 1, 2025, and after this transaction will retain 8th Avenue's nut butters, fruit, and nut products and granola businesses which are expected to be integrated into the Post Consumer Brands segment. 
  • Post expects that the nut butters, fruit, and nut products and granola businesses will contribute approximately $45-50M in adjusted EBITDA in fiscal year 2026 before the realization of cost synergies, which Post management expects to be at an annual run rate of approximately $15M by the end of fiscal year 2026. 
  • On August 27, 2025, Post's board approved a new $500M share repurchase authorization.

data.title: Kulicke and Soffa declares $0.205 dividend
data.description:
• Kulicke and Soffa (NASDAQ:KLIC) declared $0.205/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.17%
  • Payable Oct. 7; for shareholders of record Sept. 18; ex-div Sept. 18.
  • See KLIC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: PCE in charts: Annual core PCE rate ticks up in July
Autris signs term sheet to acquire bitcoin treasury company
data.description: • Autris (OTCPK:AUTR) announced on Friday the  signing of a term sheet to acquire a Bitcoin Treasury company to further enhance its Bitcoin treasury strategy.

data.title: Allegiant sees July passenger traffic increase 10.3%
data.description:
• Allegiant Travel (NASDAQ:ALGT) on Friday reported a 10.3% increase in scheduled service in July nearly to 2.09M, driving up the total number of miles flown by paying passengers (revenue passenger miles) by 10.3% to 1.86M.
  • Available seat miles rose around 11.5% to 2.14M.
  • ALGT +0.17% premarket to $62.41.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 5 stocks to watch on Friday: BABA, PEP, ULTA, APLD, CAT
data.description:
Stock index futures pared some losses on Friday, as the July PCE report came in-line. Here are five stocks to watch:

American Depositary Shares of Alibaba (BABA) shares were up 4.4% in premarket trade as the firm has reportedly developed a new chip that is more versatile than its older chips. The new chip, which is now in testing, is meant to serve a broader range of AI inference tasks, the report added.

PepsiCo (PEP) was up 0.2% in extended trading as it struck a deal to increase its ownership stake in Celsius Holdings through a $585 million deal. Sources indicate that the move will see PepsiCo's (PEP) stake grow to 11% after the purchase of convertible preferred stock, and the food and beverage giant will have the right to nominate another director to the Celsius board of directors.

Ulta Beauty (ULTA) shares were up 3.6% in premarket trade after the beauty retailer raised its outlook for the full year, reflecting another better-than-expected quarter and progress in its “Ulta Beauty Unleashed” strategy.

Applied Digital (APLD) was up 2.2% before the market open, as it has signed a new long-term lease with CoreWeave (CRWV) for 150MW of additional data center capacity at its Polaris Forge 1 Campus in Ellendale, North Dakota. This deal increases Applied Digital’s total expected contracted lease revenue to about $11 billion, with CoreWeave now set to occupy 400MW across three buildings at the site.

Caterpillar (CAT) fell 2.4% in premarket trade after disclosing it now expects the net impact from tariffs in the $1.5B-$1.8B range this year, up from a previous estimate of $1.3B-$1.5B, as well as a $500M-$600M impact in Q3, compared to its earlier outlook for $400M-$500M.
